homeSubtitle: Authenticate for another app, or use this iPhone as an NFC reader for Mac.
homeIdentifyDesc: Demo Bürgerportal is waiting for your ID card.
homeIdleBody: When a website or app asks you to identify with your ID card, the request will show up here.
homeCompanionDesc: Connect a paired Mac — manage devices in Settings
homeCompanionDescPair: Show a pairing code for your Mac
homePersonalDataDesc: See what is stored on your eID card
reqElsterPurpose: Sign in to your tax account
reqElsterHomeDesc: ELSTER Online on your Mac is waiting for your eID card.
reqWinePurpose: Confirm name and legal age for an online wine order
reqWineHomeDesc: Demo Weinkeller on this iPhone needs your name and proof you are 18 or older.
identifyIntroBody: You are about to identify yourself with your ID card. Only the requested data will be shared with the service provider.
identifyConsentBody: Review who processes your data and which fields will be read from your ID card.
identifyProcessorRole: Technical processing of the online ID function
identifyDataItems: First name, last name, date of birth, address
personalDataIntroBody: Scan your ID to view the data stored on the chip. Nothing is sent to a service provider — results stay on this device for this session only.
personalDataResultsBody: These values were read from the chip. They are not stored after you leave this screen.
identifyNoStorage: No ID card data is stored on this device or by the processor after the transaction.
placeBody: Please place your ID card at the top of the back of the device.
placeTip: Remove any thick case if scanning fails.
nfcReadyBody: Please place your ID card at the top of the back of the device.
nfcReadingBody: Keep holding still. Do not move the card.
nfcErrorMoved: The ID card was moved away. Try again and keep it still.
nfcErrorTimeout: No card was detected in time. Try again.
nfcErrorGeneric: Something went wrong while reading. Please try again.
pinSubtitle: Enter the six-digit PIN for your ID card.
pinWrongBody: The PIN you entered is incorrect. Please try again.
pinLockedBody: Too many incorrect attempts. Use your PUK to unlock in Settings, or try again later.
pinSecureNote: Your PIN stays on this device and is never shown.
pinInfoIntro: Your card PIN protects the online ID function on your ID card. Here is what you need to know before entering it.
pinInfoPoint1: You chose this PIN yourself for the online ID function. It is not printed on the card.
pinInfoPoint2: Entering the PIN confirms that you allow the requested ID data to be shared with the service provider.
pinInfoPoint3: The PIN is verified on the ID card chip. This app never stores or displays your PIN.
pinInfoNotBank: This is only for the online ID function — not your debit card, phone, or other passwords.
pinInfoAttempts: After several wrong entries the PIN locks. You can unlock it with your PUK or at a citizens’ office.
pinInfoForgot: Set a new PIN in person at your citizens’ office (Bürgeramt). A Transport-PIN from the PIN letter is only for first-time setup.
pinInfoProcessorRole: Technical processing of the online ID function
pinInfoNoStorage: No ID card data or PIN is stored on this device or by the processor after the transaction.
successBody: Authentication completed successfully. The service provider received the requested data.
failBody: The identification could not be completed.
companionHero: Use this iPhone as an NFC reader for Mac
companionBody: Open AusweisApp on your Mac, choose “Smartphone as card reader”, then enter the pairing code shown here.
companionCodeBody: Enter this code on your Mac. Both devices must be on the same network.
companionPickBody: Choose a Mac to use this iPhone as its card reader.
companionWaitingBody: Keep this screen open. Your Mac will connect shortly.
companionNetworkNotice: Local network access must be allowed in Settings.
companionConnectedBody: This iPhone is ready. When prompted on Mac, place your ID card on the phone.
companionReadyBody: This iPhone is paired and can read your ID card when Mac asks.
companionReadyToggleBody: Stay ready so Mac can request a scan anytime.
companionWaitingForMac: Waiting for a request from Mac…
companionAuthDoneBody: Your card PIN was accepted. You can keep standing by.
checkBody: Check that this iPhone can read your ID card over NFC and that the eID function responds.
checkNfcFail: NFC not available in this browser
checkWhatNfcBody: Confirms the phone can communicate with the ID card chip.
checkWhatEidBody: Confirms the online ID function on the card can be reached.
checkSuccessBody: This device can read your ID card. You are ready to identify online or use it as a card reader.
settingsChangePinAsk: What kind of PIN do you have?
settingsChangePinTypesHelp: How do the PIN types differ?
settingsChangePinTypesBody: Your ID card may use a 6-digit PIN you chose, a one-time 5-digit Transport-PIN from the PIN letter, or no usable PIN yet.
settingsChangePinSixBody: Chosen by you or received via the PIN reset service
settingsChangePinSixHelp: The regular online ID PIN. You enter it whenever you identify online.
settingsChangePinTransportBody: Received by letter when you applied for the ID card
settingsChangePinTransportHelp: A temporary PIN from the PIN letter. Replace it once with your own 6-digit PIN.
settingsChangePinNoneHelp: If you never set a PIN or forgot it, request a new one via the PIN reset service or at your citizens’ office.
settingsChangePinNoneInfo: Set a new PIN in person at your citizens’ office (Bürgeramt), or use the official PIN reset service. A Transport-PIN from the PIN letter is only for first-time setup.
settingsChangePinDoneBody: Your new card PIN is ready to use.
settingsLanguageSystemUses: Matches the language of this iPhone
settingsUseSystemFontBody: Use the system font instead of the app font.
settingsReadingModeFooter: Choose how the ID card is read during identification.
settingsHidePinAnimations: Hide key animations during PIN entry
settingsManualRedirectBody: After identification, return to the service provider manually instead of automatically.
settingsDeviceNameFooter: This name is shown on paired Macs when this iPhone is used as a card reader.
settingsShowAuthOnDevice: Show requested authorization on this device
settingsManagePairingsFooter: Paired devices can use this iPhone as an NFC card reader.
settingsNoPairings: No paired devices yet. Add a Mac to get started.
settingsRandomizeKeysBody: Shuffle the PIN keypad layout each time you enter your PIN.
settingsHideKeyAnimationsBody: Disable visual feedback when tapping keys on the PIN pad.
settingsDetectRecordingBody: Warn or block sensitive screens while screen recording is active.
settingsPrivacyBody: Demo app — no real ID data is processed or stored.
privacyIntro: This demo explains how online identification would handle your ID card data. No real personal data is processed.
privacyProcessorRole: Provides the AusweisApp and related eID infrastructure
privacyNoStorage: ID card data is transmitted for the transaction only and is not saved on this phone or by Governikus GmbH & Co. KG after completion.
privacyContactBody: Governikus GmbH & Co. KG · Hochschulring 4 · 28359 Bremen · privacy@governikus.de
helpHubFooter: Setup, support links, diagnostics, and legal information.
helpSetupFooter: Demo only — walks through a simulated first-run check.
helpProvidersBody: Services that support online identification with AusweisApp.
helpDataFooter: Demo diagnostics for support. No real device logs are uploaded.
helpLicenseIntro: Please read the license terms before using AusweisApp. Privacy information is available on the project website.
helpLicenseC: Appendix — license texts of used open-source libraries
helpLicenseEuplBody: The AusweisApp source code is provided under the European Union Public Licence (EUPL) v1.2. This demo UI is for design exploration only.
helpReleaseUser100: Extended Help section; pairing shows a code on the phone; improved spacing and tab-bar hit testing.
helpReleaseDev100: Demo build — React + Tailwind iOS shell.
helpReleaseUser090: Card reader standby, PIN pad, and identify consent flow.
helpHowIdentifyA: Tap Identify, hold your ID to the phone, then enter your PIN.
helpHowCompanion: How do I use this as a card reader?
helpHowCompanionA: Tap Card reader, enter the code shown here on your Mac, then scan when asked.
helpNfc: Where do I hold the ID?
helpNfcA: Against the top of the phone, near the camera.
helpDemoA: No — this is a demo for design and UX only.
